theory.intro: Some problems need an extra length before you can reach the answer. Use Pythagoras' theorem once to find an intermediate side, then use that side in a second Pythagoras step. Keep the intermediate value exact and round only at the very end.
facts: A multistep problem hides a first unknown side that you must find before the side you actually want.
Find the intermediate side with $h=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}$ or $a=\sqrt{h^{2}-b^{2}}$, whichever fits.
**Keep the intermediate value exact** — carry a surd such as $\sqrt{52}$ and substitute its square in the next step.
**Round only at the final step**; rounding an intermediate value early makes the final answer inaccurate.
For a perimeter, find every missing side first, then add all the sides.
steps: Round only at the final step.

foundation.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.4]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\coordinate (R) at (6,0);\coordinate (Q) at (6,4);\coordinate (P) at (-6.66,9.98);\draw (O)--(P) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12$};\draw (P)--(Q) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (Q)--(R) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$4$};\draw (R)--(O) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$6$};\draw (O)--(Q) node[midway,below right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$y$};\draw (5.6,0)--(5.6,0.4)--(6,0.4);\coordinate (va) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(P)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(Q)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(O)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
All lengths are in centimetres. First find the intermediate side $y$, then find $x$.
foundation.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$y=\sqrt{6^{2}+4^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{52}$
2. **Find the required side**
$x=\sqrt{12^{2}+y^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{12^{2}+(\sqrt{52})^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{144+52}$
$=\sqrt{196}$
$=14\text{ cm}$

foundation.question_text:

\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.4]\coordinate (O) at (0,0);\coordinate (R) at (4,0);\coordinate (Q) at (4,7);\coordinate (P) at (-7.81,4.47);\draw (O)--(P) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$9$};\draw (P)--(Q) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (Q)--(R) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$7$};\draw (R)--(O) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$4$};\draw (O)--(Q) node[midway,below right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$y$};\draw (3.6,0)--(3.6,0.4)--(4,0.4);\coordinate (va) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(P)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(O)!0.5cm!(Q)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(O)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
All lengths are in centimetres. Find the intermediate side $y$, then find $x$. Round to $2$ decimal places.
foundation.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$y=\sqrt{4^{2}+7^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{65}$
2. **Find the required side**
$x=\sqrt{9^{2}+y^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{9^{2}+(\sqrt{65})^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{81+65}$
$=\sqrt{146}$
$\approx12.08\text{ cm}$

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.35]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (M) at (4,0);\coordinate (B) at (16,0);\coordinate (T) at (4,8);\draw (A)--(T) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (T)--(B) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$y$};\draw[dashed] (T)--(M) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$8\text{ cm}$};\draw (A)--(M) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$4\text{ cm}$};\draw (M)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12\text{ cm}$};\draw (4,0.5)--(4.5,0.5)--(4.5,0);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
This triangle is split into two right-angled triangles by its perpendicular height of $8\text{ cm}$. Find the perimeter of the whole triangle. Round to $2$ decimal places.
development.solution_text:
$x=\sqrt{4^{2}+8^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{80}$
$y=\sqrt{12^{2}+8^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{208}$
The perimeter is the sum of the two slant sides and the full base.
$P=\sqrt{80}+\sqrt{208}+16$
$\approx39.37\text{ cm}$

[/tikz]
Find the intermediate side $x$, then find $y$. Give both answers in exact form.
development.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$x=\sqrt{5^{2}+8^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{89}\text{ cm}$
2. **Find the required side**
$y=\sqrt{13^{2}-x^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{13^{2}-(\sqrt{89})^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{169-89}$
$=\sqrt{80}\text{ cm}$

development.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.3]\coordinate (A) at (0,9);\coordinate (B) at (0,0);\coordinate (C) at (12,0);\coordinate (D) at (16.2,5.6);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$9\text{ cm}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12\text{ cm}$};\draw (C)--(D) node[midway,below right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$7\text{ cm}$};\draw (D)--(A);\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$d$};\draw (0,0.6)--(0.6,0.6)--(0.6,0);\coordinate (va) at ($(C)!0.5cm!(A)$);\coordinate (vb) at ($(C)!0.5cm!(D)$);\draw (va)--($(va)+(vb)-(C)$)--(vb);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The quadrilateral $ABCD$ is split by the diagonal $d=AC$ into two right-angled triangles. Find the perimeter of $ABCD$. Round to $1$ decimal place.
development.solution_text:
$d=\sqrt{9^{2}+12^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{225}$
$=15\text{ cm}$
The fourth side $AD$ uses this diagonal.
$AD=\sqrt{15^{2}+7^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{274}$
The perimeter adds all four sides.
$P=9+12+7+\sqrt{274}$
$\approx44.6\text{ cm}$

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\draw (0,0) circle (2.5);\fill (0,0) circle (1.5pt);\draw (0,0)--(2,-1.5) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$12.5\text{ cm}$};\draw[dashed] (0,0)--(0,-1.5);\draw (-2,-1.5)--(2,-1.5);\node[above] at (-0.9,-1.5) {$20\text{ cm}$};\draw (0.25,-1.5)--(0.25,-1.25)--(0,-1.25);\draw[dashed] (0,-1.5)--(0,-2.5);\node[right] at (0.05,-2.0) {$d$};\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A cylindrical pipe of radius $12.5\text{ cm}$ holds water. The flat surface of the water is $20\text{ cm}$ wide. Find the depth $d$ of the water.
mastery.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
Half the water surface is $10\text{ cm}$, forming a right-angled triangle with the radius.
$\sqrt{12.5^{2}-10^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{56.25}$
$=7.5\text{ cm}$
This is the distance from the centre down to the water surface.
$d=12.5-7.5$
$=5\text{ cm}$

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.4]\coordinate (P) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (0,-6);\coordinate (Q) at (8,0);\draw (P)--(B) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$6\text{ cm}$};\draw (B)--(Q) node[midway,below right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$10\text{ cm}$};\draw (P)--(Q) node[midway,above,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$r$};\draw (0,-0.6)--(0.6,-0.6)--(0.6,0);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A shape is made of a right-angled triangle joined to a three-quarter circle. The triangle has a hypotenuse of $10\text{ cm}$ and one side of $6\text{ cm}$. Its third side $r$ is also the radius of the three-quarter circle. The perimeter is made from the two triangle sides of $6\text{ cm}$ and $10\text{ cm}$, one radius, and the three-quarter arc. Find the exact perimeter (leave $\pi$ in your answer).
mastery.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
$r=\sqrt{10^{2}-6^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{64}$
$=8\text{ cm}$
The arc is three-quarters of a full circle of radius $8$.
$\text{arc}=\frac{3}{4}\times2\pi\times8$
$=12\pi$
The perimeter adds the arc, one radius, and the two triangle sides.
$P=12\pi+8+6+10$
$=12\pi+24\text{ cm}$

mastery.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.9]\draw (0,0) circle (3.1);\fill (0,0) circle (1.5pt);\draw[dashed] (0,0)--(0,-1.4) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$14\text{ km}$};\draw (0,0)--(2.766,-1.4) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$31\text{ km}$};\draw (-2.766,-1.4)--(2.766,-1.4) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (0,-1.4)--(0.25,-1.4)--(0.25,-1.15)--(0,-1.15);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A cell tower is $14\text{ km}$ from a straight road and provides signal within a $31\text{ km}$ radius. Find $x$, the total length of road that lies within signal range. Round to $2$ decimal places.
mastery.solution_text:
1. **Find the intermediate side**
Half the road length forms a right-angled triangle with the $14\text{ km}$ distance and the $31\text{ km}$ radius.
$\sqrt{31^{2}-14^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{765}$
This is half of $x$, so double it.
$x=2\sqrt{765}$
$\approx55.32\text{ km}$
